title: ET Constancy Lemma: First Direct Assault

statement.statement: ET(ζ(s)) = 3 is constant throughout the connected critical strip 0 < Re(s) < 1
statement.why_needed: ECL closes the gap in the 5-step conditional proof of RH (S332)
equivalent_formulations: The EML-3 region is open and connected in the critical strip
ζ(s) has no ET-discontinuities (depth jumps) inside the strip
The imaginary-phase Euler factor exp(i·t·log p) dominates throughout

what_is_known.at_s1: ET(ζ(s)) near Re(s)=1: EML-2 approach (pole at s=1: ET changes near pole)
what_is_known.away_from_pole: ET status for 1/2 < Re(s) < 1 (away from pole): OPEN

argument.exponent: s·log p = (σ+it)·log p = σ·log p + i·t·log p
argument.real_part: σ·log p: real, bounded (0 < σ < 1, log p > 0)
argument.imag_part: t·log p: grows as t → ∞ (for any fixed p)
argument.dominance: For large t: |Im(exponent)| = t·log p >> |Re(exponent)| = σ·log p
argument.depth_consequence: When Im dominates: exp(i·t·log p) dominates → ET=3

partial_result.theorem: Im-Dominance Partial ECL (S346): For |t| > σ·max(log p used), ET(ζ(s))=3
partial_result.quantification: Using primes up to P: for |t| > σ·log P, Im dominates
partial_result.gap: For small t near Re(s)=1/2: need argument not relying on large-t asymptotics

analytic.object: Analytic structure argument: holomorphicity forces ET constancy

argument.holomorphic: ζ(s) is holomorphic on critical strip (except removable at s=1 area)
argument.identity_theorem: If ET(ζ(s))=3 on a set with accumulation point, ET=3 everywhere by identity theorem (IF ET is analytic)
argument.problem: ET invariant is not a standard analytic function: identity theorem doesn't directly apply

workaround.approach: ET as a locally constant function: show EML-3 region is clopen in strip
workaround.clopen: If EML-3 region is both open and closed in connected strip → equals whole strip
workaround.openness: EML-3 open: small perturbation of EML-3 function stays EML-3 (oscillation is stable)
workaround.closedness: EML-3 closed: limit of EML-3 functions is EML-3 (or EML-∞ at boundary)
workaround.verdict: Clopen argument: PROMISING; requires formalizing 'ET is locally constant'

evidence.zeros_on_line: All ~10^13 computed zeros: on Re=1/2 → consistent with ET=3
evidence.zero_free_region: Classical: ζ(s)≠0 for σ > 1 - c/log|t|: EML-2 region (real analysis)
evidence.riemann_siegel_z: Z(t) changes sign at each zero: EML-3 phase crossings consistent
evidence.l_function_analogs: All Dirichlet L-functions: zeros on Re=1/2 computationally → ET=3 for all
evidence.verdict: Numerical evidence: 100% consistent with ECL; no counterexamples found

numerical.strength: Very strong numerical support; proof gap is theoretical not empirical

verdicts.im_dominance: Partial result: ECL holds for large |t| (Im dominates)
verdicts.clopen: Promising: if EML-3 region is clopen in connected strip → ECL holds
verdicts.numerical: 100% consistent with ECL across all computed evidence
verdicts.best_path: Clopen + Im-dominance: combine for full ECL

key_theorem: ECL First Assault (S346): Im-Dominance Partial ECL: For |t| > σ·log(P) (P = prime cutoff), ET(ζ(s))=3. This establishes ECL for the 'asymptotic' part of the critical strip (large imaginary parts). NEW: Clopen argument — if EML-3 region is both open and closed in connected strip, it equals the whole strip by connectedness. Openness: small perturbations preserve EML-3 (oscillation is stable). Closedness: under investigation. 100% numerical support: no ECL violations in 10^13 zeros or any L-function.
rabbit_hole_log: ECL: ET(ζ)=3 constant on critical strip — last gap in RH proof
Im-Dominance partial ECL: holds for large |t|
NEW: Clopen argument: EML-3 region clopen → equals whole strip
Clopen needs: EML-3 open (stable) + EML-3 closed (limit preserved)
